title: Acronia dubbeldamii
intro: Acronia dubbeldamii, also known as Acronia dubbeldamii, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Acronia dubbeldamii include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acronia dubbeldamii include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine and as dietary supplements.
taxonomy: Acronia dubbeldamii belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family, comprising over 130 genera and more than 6,000 species.
